name: Source Fidelity review for Ssr Sparse Data Supplementation bundle
summary: Source-fidelity review found the seven staged source records and core extracted facts consistent with the cited primary/open source pages, PDFs, arXiv records, and existing branch-local extractions. Bibliographic metadata, response types, augmentation roles, and main reported metrics are sufficiently bounded for a baseline claim. Martin and Graulich remains intentionally limited to abstract-level metric detail and is carried as an open full-text numeric-extraction follow-up rather than a blocker.

corrections_applied.summary: Converted the Wolfe and Barber finding source locator from an abstract-level string to a structured full-text locator.
Set numeric_results_extracted to false so the abstract-level metric extraction is explicit.
Set numeric_results_extracted to false so full-text metric extraction remains visible as follow-up work.

findings.claim_or_issue: Martin and Graulich is supported for inclusion by the open abstract, but exact score-agreement values, sample sizes, model details, and prompt details are not yet extracted from full text.
findings.why_it_matters: The record can support a bounded non-essay augmentation example, but quantitative synthesis or method-specific comparison would overstate the current extraction depth.
findings.recommended_action: Extract full-text methods and results tables for Martin and Graulich before using the record in quantitative synthesis or detailed method comparisons.
